destinations.evidence: The port payload builder now reads RETH_MAX_FULFILLED_ASYNC_PER_BLOCK and RETH_MAX_DELIVERY_PER_BLOCK with default 64 and truncates fulfilled async and delivery-ready entry vectors before preparing per-entry payload candidates.

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port after the local parity fix: fulfilled async and delivery-ready Phase B/C entry vectors are capped with the downstream environment variable names, invalid or absent values default to 64, and truncation happens before expensive settlement preparation.

related_but_insufficient_code: Before the local parity fix, payload building collected fulfilled async entries and delivery-ready async entries and looped all of them.
The local fix now caps both entry vectors before per-entry preparation.

notes: Mapped after local parity fix: Phase B and Phase C async backlog vectors are bounded by the downstream env vars with default 64.
rationale: The diff adds environment-driven caps for fulfilled async and delivery-ready transaction collections, with default 64 and warnings when truncating, to keep unbounded async backlog from consuming the block-building budget. This result keeps the candidate as a distinct refined invariant because the bounded assignment packet does not contain a newer candidate that clearly supersedes it. Implementation-detail caveat: The default cap is an operational knob and may change with throughput requirements.
statement: Payload building must cap fulfilled async settlements and delivery-ready settlements per block using `RETH_MAX_FULFILLED_ASYNC_PER_BLOCK` and `RETH_MAX_DELIVERY_PER_BLOCK`, defaulting each cap to 64.
suggested_falsification_tests: Set `RETH_MAX_FULFILLED_ASYNC_PER_BLOCK=2` and provide more than two fulfilled async entries; assert only two are processed for the block. Repeat for `RETH_MAX_DELIVERY_PER_BLOCK`.
summary: Payload building caps fulfilled and delivery-ready async entries per block with the downstream env vars and default 64.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Focused payload-builder unit tests cover cap parsing defaults, configured values, and truncation behavior.
title: Async backlog per block is capped
